title: Committed review names unchanged overlap rows explicitly before routed roles

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark the stronger determinism requirement that overlap coverage be materialized as explicit unchanged file-path rows before routed roles begin.
provenance.genericCondition: Synthetic fixture models a PR where the critical determinism requirement is not just scanning overlap surfaces, but naming the unchanged overlap rows concretely in the router-owned coverage plan so reruns cannot omit them silently.

description: A sanitized committed-review scenario where a new provider resource overlaps multiple unchanged sibling surfaces. The correct review must use the review-coordinator layer to materialize those unchanged siblings as explicit file-path overlap rows before routed analysis begins.

scope.notes: Synthetic fixture models a PR where the critical determinism requirement is not just scanning overlap surfaces, but naming the unchanged overlap rows concretely in the router-owned coverage plan so reruns cannot omit them silently.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should name unchanged overlap surfaces by file path rather than referring to overlap only as a category or family.
The committed review should present routed-role work only after the deterministic coverage plan has explicitly named the required overlap rows.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not rely only on generic overlap language that omits explicit unchanged sibling file paths from the deterministic coverage plan.

notes: Use this case to benchmark the stronger determinism requirement that overlap coverage be materialized as explicit unchanged file-path rows before routed roles begin.
